Class EntityTableBuilder<T>

java.lang.Object
com.hogwai.dynamodb.simplified.entity.EntityTableBuilder<T>
Type Parameters:
T - the entity type
public final class EntityTableBuilder<T> extends Object
Builder for creating EntityTable instances for single-table design.

The entity class must be annotated with @Entity and may use @KeyComponent and @KeyPrefix annotations to define the entity schema.

Usage: 

EntityTable<User> userTable = new EntityTableBuilder<>(User.class)
        .dynamoDbClient(dynamoDbClient)
        .build();

Alternatively, use DynamoSimplifiedClient.entityTable(Class) for a simpler end-to-end flow.

    • EntityTableBuilder

      public EntityTableBuilder(@NonNull Class<T> entityClass)
      Creates a new builder for the given entity class.

  • Method Details

    • dynamoDbClient

      public EntityTableBuilder<T> dynamoDbClient(@NonNull software.amazon.awssdk.services.dynamodb.DynamoDbClient client)
      Sets the low-level DynamoDbClient to use.
      Parameters:
      client - the DynamoDB client
      Returns:
      this builder for chaining

    • enhancedClient

      public EntityTableBuilder<T> enhancedClient(@NonNull software.amazon.awssdk.enhanced.dynamodb.DynamoDbEnhancedClient client)
      Sets the DynamoDbEnhancedClient to use.

      If not set, one will be built from the provided dynamoDbClient(DynamoDbClient).

      Parameters:
      client - the enhanced DynamoDB client
      Returns:
      this builder for chaining

    • build

      public @NonNull EntityTable<T> build()
      Builds the EntityTable instance.

      Reads the entity schema from annotations, creates the underlying DynamoDB table mapping, and wraps it in an DefaultEntityTable.

      Returns:
      a new EntityTable<T>
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the entity class is not annotated with @Entity
      NullPointerException - if no DynamoDbClient is set
